metadata.description: Evaluation tasks for MCP vs. Skill agent tool integration comparison
metadata.note: Issue and PR numbers are based on the setup_github.sh output. Adjust expected_output values if your actual numbers differ.

tasks.description: How many open issues in this repo are labeled `bug`?

expected_output.explanation: Open bugs: OTLP timeout (#49), Batch shutdown (#52), Config crash (#57), CI Python 3.12 (#58), JSON unicode (#60).

tasks.description: Who is assigned to issue #52 (Batch exporter drops last batch on shutdown)?

expected_output.explanation: No one is assigned — the setup script's assignee calls silently fail because the users don't exist on GitHub.

tasks.description: What is the title of the most recently opened pull request?

expected_output.explanation: PR #63 is the most recently created PR.

tasks.description: Is issue #55 (Typo in README.md) assigned to anyone?

expected_output.explanation: The README typo issue has no assignee.

tasks.description: What labels are on the issue titled 'OTLP exporter fails silently on connection timeout'?

expected_output.explanation: The OTLP timeout issue has bug and exporter labels.

tasks.description: List all open issues labeled `bug` and check if any of them have a linked pull request. Which bugs have fixes in progress?

bugs_with_prs: Batch exporter drops last batch on shutdown
CI workflow doesn't run on Python 3.12
bugs_without_prs: OTLP exporter fails silently on connection timeout
Config file parser crashes on empty values
JSON file exporter doesn't handle unicode properly

expected_output.explanation: The batch shutdown bug has PR #61, the CI bug has PR #62. The OTLP timeout and config crash bugs have no PRs.

tasks.description: Look at the last 10 merged PRs. What percentage of them modified files in the `tests/` directory?

expected_output.explanation: setup_github.sh creates 3 open PRs but no merged PRs. The agent should report 0 merged PRs.

tasks.description: Find all open issues assigned to Alice. How many are bugs vs. enhancements?

expected_output.explanation: No issues are assigned to Alice — the setup script's assignee calls silently fail because the users don't exist on GitHub.

tasks.description: Which open issues are part of the v2.0 milestone? List them with their titles and labels.

value.title: Add support for custom span attributes
Add retry configuration to client constructor
Add gRPC transport option for OTLP exporter

expected_output.explanation: Four open issues are in the v2.0 milestone.

tasks.description: Are there any open PRs that don't have at least one review? List them.

expected_output.explanation: PR #61 has an approval from Bob. PRs #62 and #63 have no reviews.

tasks.description: Find all issues labeled `exporter`. How many are open vs. closed?

open_titles: OTLP exporter fails silently on connection timeout
Console exporter output is not configurable
Add gRPC transport option for OTLP exporter
JSON file exporter doesn't handle unicode properly

expected_output.explanation: 4 open exporter issues, 3 closed (OTLP batch drop, JSON file exporter added, console exporter added).

tasks.description: Create a new issue titled 'Add request timeout configuration' with the label `enhancement`, and add it to the v2.0 milestone.

checks: Issue exists with exact title 'Add request timeout configuration'
Issue is in the v2.0 milestone

tasks.description: The word 'recieve' appears in README.md. Create a branch called `fix/readme-typo`, fix the typo, commit with an appropriate message, push, and open a PR that references issue #55.

checks: A PR exists from fix/readme-typo to main
PR body or title references issue #55
Commit message is descriptive (e.g., 'fix: correct typo in README.md')
expected_output.note: Issue #55 is the typo issue. Agent should reference it.

tasks.description: Add the label `needs-triage` to issues #57 and #60.

expected_output.note: Issue #57 is the config crash bug, #60 is the unicode bug.

tasks.description: Close issue #55 (the README typo) with a comment saying 'Fixed in PR #X' (use the actual PR number if one exists, otherwise note that no PR was found).

checks: Issue #55 has a comment referencing a PR number or stating no PR exists
expected_output.note: If T13 was run first, a PR exists. Otherwise, the agent should note no PR was found.

tasks.description: Add a review comment to PR #62 saying 'LGTM — just verify the 3.12 tests pass on the ARM runner too.'

checks: PR #62 has a review or comment containing the specified text

tasks.description: Create a new label called `performance` with color `#ff9900` and description 'Performance-related issues', then apply it to issue #53.

expected_output.note: Issue #53 is the retry config enhancement.

tasks.description: Generate a changelog for everything merged between tag v1.0.0 and v1.1.0, grouped by category (features, bug fixes, docs). Format as Markdown.

criteria: Contains a Features section listing: JSON file exporter, console exporter, batch processing, gzip compression
Contains a Bug Fixes section listing: OTLP batch size fix, batch shutdown race condition, nested attribute serialization
Contains a Docs section listing: exporters guide, API reference, examples
Formatted as valid Markdown with headers and lists
Derived from actual git log between v1.0.0 and v1.1.0 tags

tasks.description: Which contributor has the most commits to files in the `src/acme_sdk/exporters/` directory? What percentage of total commits to that directory are theirs?

expected_output.explanation: Alice has the most commits to the exporters directory (OTLP, JSON, console exporters). Dana contributed the OTLP batch fix and unicode fix.

tasks.description: Identify the 3 files with the most commits in the entire repo history. For each, summarize the types of changes (new features, bug fixes, refactors).

criteria: Identifies 3 files with the most commits (likely: __init__.py, CHANGELOG.md, client.py or otlp.py, batching.py, auth.py)
For each file, provides a reasonable summary of change types
Change type classifications are accurate (features vs fixes vs refactors)
Uses git log to determine commit counts

tasks.description: Audit the open PRs: for each one, report whether it has reviews, whether CI passed, whether there are merge conflicts, and how old it is.

criteria: Lists all 3 open PRs
Reports review status: PR #61 approved, PR #62 no reviews, PR #63 no reviews (draft)
Reports CI status for each PR
Reports merge conflict status
Reports age or creation date for each PR

tasks.description: Find all issues that were opened and closed within the same milestone. What was the average time from open to close?

criteria: Identifies closed issues in v1.0.0 and v1.1.0 milestones
Computes time from open to close for each
Reports average time (likely very short since issues were created and closed in the same script run)
Notes that the timestamps may be artificial

tasks.description: Which directories in `src/` have zero test coverage? (i.e., no corresponding test file exists in `tests/`)

value.explanation: No test_console.py, no test_serialization.py, no test_config.py exist in the tests directory.

criteria: Correctly identifies source files without corresponding test files
Does not falsely report covered files as uncovered
Examines the actual test directory structure

tasks.description: Generate a contributor activity summary: for each contributor, show total commits, most recent commit date, and which areas of the codebase they primarily work in.

criteria: Lists all 4 contributors: Alice Chen, Bob Martinez, Charlie Kim, Dana Okafor
Shows commit counts (Alice ~20+, Bob ~10+, Charlie ~5+, Dana ~5+)
Shows most recent commit date for each
Describes primary work areas: Alice=src/, Bob=docs/examples, Charlie=CI/config, Dana=bug fixes

tasks.description: Compare the v1.0.0 and v1.1.0 releases: how many issues were resolved in each, how many PRs merged, and what areas of the codebase were most changed?

criteria: Reports issues in v1.0.0 milestone (3 bugs resolved) or similar
Reports issues in v1.1.0 milestone (2 bugs, 4 enhancements resolved) or similar
Analyzes git diff between tags to identify most-changed areas
Notes that v1.1.0 was larger in scope (more files, more features)
Key areas changed: exporters/ (new in v1.1.0), utils/batching.py, auth.py
